elements.name: intro_selfpaced_pd_equity_post
elements.html:
Equity in CS Self-paced Online Professional Learning for Educators

Post-Module Survey

Code.org relies on surveys as a crucial tool to stay connected to teachers, gather feedback about our curriculum and your teaching experience, and to improve our programs.

Privacy and Confidentiality

  • Your individual responses here are completely confidential.
  • Your identity will never be revealed publicly or used to tie any responses back to you personally.
  • We may share anonymous, aggregate data (representing all responses) publicly.
  • Your anonymized responses will be used by Code.org for program improvement.

Your honesty and candor are appreciated.

Equity in CS - Post-Module Survey

elements.name: section1_effectiveness
elements.html:
The questions in this section aim to understand how valuable was this self-paced learning experience for you as an educator. Please indicate your level of agreement with each statement

.

elements.name: good_use_of_time
elements.title: Engaging with the Equity in CS professional learning module was a good use of my time.

elements.name: nps_self_paced_pd
elements.title: How likely are you to recommend this self-paced professional learning experience to a friend or colleague?

elements.name: plan_implement
elements.title: I plan to use what I learned in the Equity in CS module in my role as an educator.

elements.name: equity_comfort_ability_implement
elements.title: After completing this module, I am more confident in my ability to identify and address inequities within my computer science classroom and/or school.

elements.name: content_relevant_role
elements.title: The content of the module was relevant to my role.

elements.name: equity_concepts_strategies
elements.title: After participating in the Equity in CS module, I feel comfortable with my ability to implement strategies related to these concepts in my role as a  CS educator

elements.name: content_to_improve_relevance
elements.title: What specific topics or examples would you like to see added to this unit to make it more relevant to your teaching?

elements.name: barriers_implementation
elements.title: What, if any, are the main barriers to using what you’ve learned?

choices.text: I am not currently teaching computer science.
It is not clear to me how to apply what I’ve learned.
I need to learn more about Equity in CS before using it in my classroom.
It is not supported by school/district administration.
I lack the time to implement these strategies.
I need more examples that are relevant to my grade level/subject area.

elements.name: section2_perceptions_structure
elements.html: The questions in this section aim to understand your experience with this self-paced learning module. Please indicate your level of agreement with each statement.

elements.name: information_clarity
elements.title: The information presented in this module was clear and easy to understand.

elements.name: easy_navigation
elements.title: Course navigation was clear and easy to follow.

elements.name: easy_navigation_improvement
elements.title: How can course navigation could be improved.?

elements.name: pacing
elements.title: The pacing was appropriate for a self-paced learning experience.

elements.name: module_progression
elements.title: The module progressed in a logical order.

elements.name: module_progression_improvement
elements.title: How could the module's progression and structure be improved?

elements.name: amount_reading_material
elements.title: The amount of reading material was appropriate for this module.

elements.name: equity_guide_frequency_use
elements.title: I often used the Equity in CS activity guide while progressing through the module.

elements.name: equity_guide_feedback
elements.title: Please briefly explain what you found most effective about the activity guide, and what may need improvement.

elements.name: equity_supported_learning
elements.title: Which of the following components of the module supported your learning the most?
elements.description: Rank the options from most helpful (5) to least helpful (1).

rows.text: Reading a page that contained text and images about a topic.
Making a plan to foster equitable access, diversity, and inclusion in my CS classroom.
Writing reflections on experiences and teaching practices.
Sharing strategies in the Code.org forum.

elements.name: engagement_components
elements.title: Is there any feedback you would like to share about what made these components more or less engaging?

elements.name: how_long_to_complete
elements.title: How long did you spend completing this module?

elements.name: section3_content
elements.html: The questions in this section aim to understand your perceptions about the content by creating some opportunities for reflection.

elements.name: changes_teaching_practices
elements.title: What, if any, specific changes do you plan to make in your teaching practice as a result of this module?

elements.name: significant_learning_experience
elements.title: Can you tell us about a time when you had an 'aha' moment or a significant learning experience during the module?

elements.name: concepts_challenge_understanding
elements.title: Were there any concepts presented in the module that challenged your current understanding of equity in CS?

elements.name: ongoing_support
elements.title: What kind of ongoing support would be helpful for you to continue implementing inclusive practices in your CS teaching?

elements.name: other_feedback
elements.title: Do you have any feedback about this module that you would like to share with the Code.org team?

elements.name: permission_to_quote
elements.title: I give Code.org permission to quote my written comments from this survey for use on social media, promotional materials, and other communications.

choices.text: Yes, I give Code.org permission to quote me and use my name.
Yes, I give Code.org permission to quote me, but I want to be anonymous.
No, I do not give Code.org my permission.

elements.name: equity_participate_focus_group
elements.title: OPTIONAL: Would you be willing to participate in a follow-up focus group to provide direct feedback about this online course?

elements.name: equity_email_focus_group
elements.title: Great! Please share an email address where we may contact you.

elements.name: thank_you
elements.html:
Thank You!

Thanks from Code.org for your time, thoughts, and passion for teaching.
